title: Reconciliation panel partial and route
analysis: Task 21 requires creating the reconciliation panel HTML partial template and its FastAPI route. The dashboard already has: (1) a fully implemented data layer in dashboard/src/dashboard/data/reconciliation.py with 5 async query functions (get_recent_runs, get_watermarks, get_buffer_stats, get_burst_state, get_latest_verdict), (2) comprehensive tests for those data functions in test_reconciliation.py, (3) a base.html template with Tailwind CSS + htmx + Alpine.js and dark theme conventions, (4) a DashboardConfig with a reconciliation_db property deriving the DB path from project_root, (5) conftest fixtures including reconciliation_db with full sample data and client for TestClient. What's missing: the templates/partials/ directory, the recon.html partial template, the /partials/recon route in app.py, a timeago Jinja2 filter, and a config instance in app.py. The route will call all 5 data functions with config.reconciliation_db and pass results to the template. The partial is a standalone HTML fragment (loaded via htmx, not extending base.html) with a two-column grid: left column shows buffer stats, burst state, watermarks, and verdict; right column shows a recent runs table. Tests will mock the 5 data functions to avoid needing a real SQLite DB, verifying route returns 200 with expected HTML structure, badges, and empty-state fallbacks.

prerequisites.description: Create templates/partials/ directory at dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/partials/ so the recon.html template can be placed there

steps.description: Write tests for timeago filter function in dashboard/tests/test_recon_panel.py class TestTimeagoFilter. Tests: (1) test_none_returns_never — timeago(None) returns 'never', (2) test_minutes_ago — ISO timestamp ~3 minutes ago returns '3m ago', (3) test_hours_ago — ISO timestamp ~2 hours ago returns '2h ago', (4) test_days_ago — ISO timestamp ~2 days ago returns '2d ago', (5) test_invalid_string_returns_never — timeago('not-a-date') returns 'never', (6) test_empty_string_returns_never — timeago('') returns 'never'. Import timeago from dashboard.app.

Implement timeago filter function in dashboard/src/dashboard/app.py. Function signature: def timeago(value: str | None) -> str. If value is None, return 'never'. Try datetime.fromisoformat(value); on ValueError/TypeError, return 'never'. Ensure timezone-aware (add UTC if naive). Compute delta = datetime.now(UTC) - parsed. Return 'Xd ago' if delta >= 24h, 'Xh ago' if delta >= 60m, 'Xm ago' otherwise (minimum 0m).

Write test in TestTimeagoFilter: test_filter_registered_on_jinja2_env — import templates from dashboard.app, assert 'timeago' in templates.env.filters and templates.env.filters['timeago'] is the timeago function.
Register the timeago function as a Jinja2 filter on the templates environment in app.py. Add line: templates.env.filters['timeago'] = timeago (after both templates and timeago are defined).

Write tests for GET /partials/recon route with populated mock data in class TestReconRoute. Use unittest.mock.patch as decorators or context managers to mock all 5 data functions at the dashboard.app module level (e.g., patch('dashboard.app.get_buffer_stats', new_callable=AsyncMock)). Mock return values: get_buffer_stats → {'buffered_count': 3, 'oldest_event_age_seconds': 600.0}, get_burst_state → [{'agent_id': 'agent-1', 'state': 'bursting', 'last_write_at': '2026-03-19T00:00:00+00:00', 'burst_started_at': '2026-03-19T00:00:00+00:00'}, {'agent_id': 'agent-2', 'state': 'idle', 'last_write_at': '2026-03-19T00:00:00+00:00', 'burst_started_at': None}], get_watermarks → {'last_full_run_completed': '2026-03-19T10:00:00+00:00', 'last_episode_timestamp': '2026-03-19T10:30:00+00:00', 'last_memory_timestamp': '2026-03-19T10:40:00+00:00', 'last_task_change_timestamp': '2026-03-19T10:50:00+00:00'}, get_latest_verdict → {'run_id': 'run-001', 'severity': 'ok', 'action_taken': 'none', 'reviewed_at': '2026-03-19T10:00:00+00:00'}, get_recent_runs → [{'id': 'run-001', 'run_type': 'full', 'trigger_reason': 'staleness_timer', 'started_at': '2026-03-19T08:00:00+00:00', 'completed_at': '2026-03-19T08:05:00+00:00', 'events_processed': 7, 'status': 'completed', 'duration_seconds': 300.0}]. Assertions: (a) response status 200, (b) content-type includes text/html, (c) HTML contains '3 events buffered', (d) contains 'agent-1' and 'agent-2', (e) contains burst state badge indicators (text 'bursting' and 'idle'), (f) contains watermark labels 'Last full run' and 'Last episode' and 'Last memory' and 'Last task change', (g) contains verdict severity 'bg-green-600' badge class (for 'ok'), (h) contains 'none' (action_taken), (i) contains runs table content: 'staleness_timer', 'completed', 'bg-green-600', (j) contains 'grid grid-cols-2' layout class.

Implement GET /partials/recon async route in app.py and create templates/partials/recon.html. In app.py: import the 5 data functions from dashboard.data.reconciliation, import DashboardConfig from dashboard.config, import Request from fastapi. Route handler: create config = DashboardConfig.from_env(), db = config.reconciliation_db, await all 5 functions concurrently with asyncio.gather, render templates.TemplateResponse('partials/recon.html', context). Template: two-column grid (grid grid-cols-2 gap-6). Left column cards (bg-gray-800 rounded-lg p-6): (1) Event Buffer — 'N events buffered', oldest age formatted as Xm Xs with text-yellow-400 if >300s; (2) Burst State — table with agent_id + state badges (bg-orange-600 for bursting, bg-gray-600 for idle), 'No agents' fallback; (3) Watermarks — 4 rows with text-gray-400 labels and text-white values using timeago filter; (4) Latest Verdict — severity badge (bg-green-600=ok, bg-yellow-600=minor, bg-orange-600=moderate, bg-red-600=serious) + action_taken, 'No verdicts yet' fallback. Right column card: Recent Runs table with columns Trigger, Type, Status, Events, Duration, When. Status badges (bg-green-600=completed, bg-blue-600=running, bg-red-600=failed, bg-orange-600=rolled_back, bg-purple-600=circuit_breaker). Duration in seconds or '—'. When using timeago. 'No reconciliation runs yet' fallback.

Write tests for GET /partials/recon with empty/default data in class TestReconRouteEmpty. Same mock pattern but with empty returns: get_buffer_stats → {'buffered_count': 0, 'oldest_event_age_seconds': None}, get_burst_state → [], get_watermarks → {}, get_latest_verdict → None, get_recent_runs → []. Assertions: (a) response status 200, (b) HTML contains '0 events buffered', (c) contains 'No agents', (d) contains 'No verdicts yet', (e) contains 'No reconciliation runs yet', (f) does NOT contain 'bg-green-600' or 'bg-red-600' (no verdict or run badges when empty).

Verify and fix template empty state handling. Ensure all Jinja2 conditionals are correct: {% if burst_state %}...{% else %}No agents{% endif %}, {% if verdict %}...{% else %}No verdicts yet{% endif %}, {% if runs %}...{% else %}No reconciliation runs yet{% endif %}, {% if watermarks %}...{% else %} show 'never' for all 4 fields {% endif %}. These should mostly be covered by step-6 implementation but this step ensures the empty-state tests from step-7 pass. Run full test suite to confirm all tests green.

design_decisions.decision: Create DashboardConfig.from_env() inside the route handler, not as module-level state
design_decisions.rationale: The app currently has no config instance. Creating it in the handler is simplest, keeps state local, and is easily testable (the data functions are mocked so the config's DB path is never actually used in tests). A module-level config or FastAPI dependency can be introduced later when more routes need it.

design_decisions.decision: Place timeago filter function directly in app.py alongside the filter registration
design_decisions.rationale: Only one custom filter is needed. The task explicitly says to register it on the templates environment in app.py. A separate filters.py would be premature — refactor when more filters are added.

design_decisions.decision: Mock the 5 data functions in route tests rather than using real SQLite fixtures
design_decisions.rationale: The data functions are already thoroughly tested in test_reconciliation.py with full SQLite fixtures. Route tests should verify route+template integration (HTTP contract, HTML structure, badge rendering), not re-test the data layer. Mocking gives precise control over rendering scenarios and avoids the complexity of wiring a temp DB into the app's config.

design_decisions.decision: Partial template is a standalone HTML fragment, not extending base.html
design_decisions.rationale: Per the PRD and htmx architecture, partials are loaded into the page via htmx swaps. They must be HTML fragments without <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, <head>, or <body> tags. The base.html template with full document structure is only for full page renders.

design_decisions.decision: Mock data functions at the dashboard.app import location, not at dashboard.data.reconciliation
design_decisions.rationale: The route in app.py will import the functions directly (from dashboard.data.reconciliation import ...). Patching at 'dashboard.app.get_recent_runs' etc. ensures the mock applies to the actual references used in the route handler.

design_decisions.decision: Use text-yellow-400 for buffer age warning when oldest_event_age_seconds > 300
design_decisions.rationale: Task specifies 'yellow text if age > 300s'. Tailwind's text-yellow-400 provides clearly visible yellow on the dark bg-gray-800 card background while maintaining readability.

design_decisions.decision: Use asyncio.gather for concurrent data fetching in the route handler
design_decisions.rationale: All 5 data queries are independent SQLite reads. Running them concurrently with asyncio.gather minimizes route latency instead of awaiting each sequentially.

design_decisions.decision: Format buffer age as 'Xm Xs' inline in the template rather than through timeago filter
design_decisions.rationale: The buffer age is a float (seconds), not an ISO timestamp. The timeago filter is designed for ISO strings. The template will compute minutes/seconds from the float directly using Jinja2 arithmetic: {{ (age // 60)|int }}m {{ (age % 60)|int }}s.

reuse.what: 5 async reconciliation query functions (get_recent_runs, get_watermarks, get_buffer_stats, get_burst_state, get_latest_verdict)
reuse.how: Import all 5 functions and call them with config.reconciliation_db in the route handler. They return dicts/lists ready for template rendering.
Use DashboardConfig.from_env() to get config instance in route handler, access config.reconciliation_db for the SQLite DB path passed to all 5 query functions.
Use existing `templates` variable for TemplateResponse rendering and register timeago filter on templates.env.filters dict.

reuse.what: Dark theme Tailwind CSS conventions from base.html
reuse.how: Follow established dark palette: bg-gray-900 page background, bg-gray-800 for cards, text-gray-100 for primary text, text-gray-400 for labels, border-gray-700 for borders. These are visually consistent with the base template's header and body styling.

reuse.what: TestClient fixture and class-based test organization
reuse.how: Use existing `client` fixture for route tests. Follow same class-based test organization (TestClassName with descriptive method names) and import-inside-test pattern used throughout test_scaffold.py and test_reconciliation.py.

reuse.what: conftest reconciliation_db fixture with sample data
reuse.how: Available as reference for mock data shapes — the fixture inserts sample runs, events, burst state, watermarks, and verdicts that document the exact data structures the template must handle.
